title: Ensure log monitoring and alerts are set up for Cloud Firewall  changes

input_row.title: Ensure log monitoring and alerts are set up for Cloud Firewall  changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for Cloud Firewall rule changes.
input_row.rationale: Monitoring for Create or Update firewall rule events gives insight network access changes and may reduce the time it takes to detect suspicious activity.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to ensure the log monitoring and alerts are set up for Cloud Firewall Changes: 1. Logon to SLS Console. 2. Click Log Service Audit Service in the navigation pane to go to the Log Service Audit Service page. 3. Ensure the Action Trail are Enabled under the Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration page, and click Central Project. 4. Select Alerts. 5. Ensure below alert rule has been enabled and saved in the target actiontrail_log "event.serviceName": "Cloudfw" and ("event.eventName": CreateVpcFirewallControlPolicy or "event.eventName": DeleteVpcFirewallControlPolicy or "event.eventName": ModifyVpcFirewallControlPolicy) | select count(1) as c
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to ensure the log monitoring and alerts are set up Cloud Firewall Changes: 1. Logon to SLS Console. 2. Click Log Service Audit Service in the navigation pane. 3. Go to Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration page. a. Select a location of project for logs. 74 | P a g e b. Check the Action Trail and configure a proper days. c. Click Save to save the changes. 4. Go to Access to Cloud Products > Global Configurations click Central Project. 5. Select Log Management > Actiontrail Log. 6. In the search/analytics console, input below query "event.serviceName": "Cloudfw" and ("event.eventName": CreateVpcFirewallControlPolicy or "event.eventName": DeleteVpcFirewallControlPolicy or "event.eventName": ModifyVpcFirewallControlPolicy) | select count(1) as c 7. Create a dashboard and set alert for the query result.
input_row.default_value: The monitoring dashboard and alert is not set by default

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_sls_log_service_cloudfirewall_changes_monitoring_alerts

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- Use the Alibaba Cloud SDK to call the appropriate logging APIs to verify that the logging and alerting for Cloud Firewall changes are configured.
- APIs to check:
  - Use `DescribeLogstore` to verify if the log store for the Action Trail includes the necessary filters for Cloud Firewall changes.
  - Use `ListAlertRules` to check if an alert rule exists that matches the filter criteria for Cloud Firewall changes.
- Use the following Aliyun CLI commands:
  - `aliyun sls GetLogstore --project your_project_name --logstore your_logstore_name`
    - Verify that the necessary logging is enabled and the relevant filters are active.
  - `aliyun sls ListAlertRules --project your_project_name`
    - Check for the existence of alert rules for events related to `CreateVpcFirewallControlPolicy`, `DeleteVpcFirewallControlPolicy`, and `ModifyVpcFirewallControlPolicy`.
  
- Specific configuration properties/settings to validate:
  - Ensure the log monitoring rule is set with the condition: `event.serviceName: Cloudfw` and `(event.eventName: CreateVpcFirewallControlPolicy or event.eventName: DeleteVpcFirewallControlPolicy or event.eventName: ModifyVpcFirewallControlPolicy)`.
  
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:
  - Pass: If the log store is configured correctly, and at least one alert rule exists that tracks the relevant Cloud Firewall change events.
  - Fail: If the log store configurations do not exist or the alert rules are not set.

This automated approach provides a confidence that compliance can be continuously monitored without manual intervention, relying entirely on API checks.
